key_insight: A hybrid IFENN model can learn phase-field fracture initiation and crack growth with a once-trained neural surrogate coupled to FEM, enabling generalizable predictions across new geometries.
engineering_relevance: This offers a lower-cost path to simulate fracture evolution in solid mechanics while preserving physics-based equilibrium and improving applicability beyond the training case.
method: FEM handles mechanical equilibrium in the coupled solver.
DeepOKAN predicts phase-field diffusion during crack initiation; CNN handles propagation.
Both networks are trained once on a benchmark geometry using physics-informed sampling from the fracture process zone.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports accurate and flexible performance on both the training geometry and unseen geometries, using only a small number of training increments and strategically sampled Gauss points.
watch_item: Extrapolation in far-field regions requires artificial boundary conditions, so deployment must carefully enforce near-zero phase-field behavior away from the crack tip.
paper_title: A hybrid IFENN solver for generalizable modeling of phase-field fracture initiation and propagation
